name: Chili Piper API
what_it_does: Chili Piper API — inbound lead routing and meeting scheduling platform that instantly books demos with qualified prospects directly from web forms, enabling agents to configure routing rules, manage meeting types, and track booking analytics.
use_cases: Agents configuring Chili Piper routing rules to direct inbound leads to the correct sales rep based on territory or account
Booking analytics — agents querying Chili Piper meeting data for no-show rates, conversion metrics, and rep performance
CRM sync automation — agents ensuring Chili Piper booking data flows correctly into Salesforce or HubSpot
Meeting type management — agents creating and updating meeting configurations for product launches or seasonal campaigns
Lead qualification — agents reading Chili Piper form submissions and routing decisions for pipeline analysis
not_for: External self-scheduling (non-sales) — use Calendly or Cal.com for general-purpose external booking pages
Internal team scheduling — use Reclaim.ai or Google Calendar for internal meeting coordination
SMB budgets — Chili Piper is enterprise-priced at ~$150-225/user/month
best_when: You have a high-volume inbound motion and need to instantly route and book demos from web forms to the right sales rep to maximize conversion rates.
avoid_when: You need general-purpose scheduling or have a small sales team — Calendly at $10-20/user/month is far more cost-effective.

auth.notes: API key from Chili Piper admin settings. Key has workspace-level access. No scope granularity. REST API primarily for configuration and analytics; booking embed uses JavaScript SDK.

pricing.paid_tiers: Instant Booker: ~$150/user/month; Distro: ~$225/user/month; Concierge: custom enterprise pricing
pricing.notes: Enterprise pricing with annual contracts. Demo required before pricing. API access included in plans. Significant investment relative to Calendly — justified by inbound conversion lift.

performance.rate_limits: No published rate limits; API intended for configuration, not high-frequency queries

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API documentation is sparse — primarily designed for sales team use, not developer use. Error responses are basic. Webhook documentation is more comprehensive than REST API docs. Configuration changes may take time to propagate.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Configuration updates are idempotent. Meeting bookings are not idempotent — duplicate bookings can occur without deduplication.
known_agent_gotchas: Primary integration is JavaScript embed, not REST API — most features require client-side implementation
CRM integration (Salesforce/HubSpot) must be pre-configured by admin — API cannot set up CRM connections
Routing rules are complex — territory, round-robin, and account-based routing require careful configuration
API documentation is limited compared to enterprise pricing — expect to rely on support for edge cases
Rate limits undocumented — API is not designed for high-frequency programmatic access

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II. GDPR compliant with EU data processing options. API key with workspace-wide access and no scope granularity. US and EU data hosting. Enterprise security review available.
